matchups.deciding_factor: Duke's ~5-point AdjEM advantage (+38.90 vs +33.5) and #1 overall offensive efficiency, anchored by Cameron Boozer's matchup-proof scoring (22.5 PPG, 56.5% FG, 40.9% 3PT). Duke's elite ball security (3rd nationally in A/TO ratio, 1.82) limits Houston's ability to generate the turnovers their defensive scheme depends on.

Data agents split (Statistical 54% Arizona, Matchup 57% Michigan). Statistical below 56% threshold — tiebreaker void. Agents 2-2. Situational tiebreaker: efficiency metrics essentially tied (AdjEM +37.66 vs +37.58), but Michigan is missing two rotation players (Grady OUT, Cason OUT) while Arizona is at full strength. Roster availability breaks the tie.
